# Compile from `pyproject.toml`

`pyproject.toml` is the [PEP 621](https://peps.python.org/pep-0621/) standard for declaring project
metadata. `pip-compile` reads dependencies straight from the `[project]` table.

## A minimal project

```toml
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=63"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[project]
name = "my-app"
version = "1.0"
requires-python = ">=3.10"
dependencies = ["django"]
```

Compile to `requirements.txt`:

```console
pip-compile -o requirements.txt pyproject.toml
```

The output:

```text
#
# This file is autogenerated by pip-compile with Python 3.13
# by the following command:
#
#    pip-compile --output-file=requirements.txt pyproject.toml
#
asgiref==3.7.2
    # via django
django==4.2.7
    # via my-app (pyproject.toml)
sqlparse==0.4.4
    # via django
```

## Optional dependencies

Optional dependencies under `[project.optional-dependencies]` map to extras. Compile each extra into a
separate file when you want a separate install target:

```toml
[project]
name = "my-app"
version = "1.0"
dependencies = ["django"]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = ["pytest", "ipython"]
docs = ["sphinx"]
```

```console
pip-compile -o requirements.txt pyproject.toml
pip-compile --extra dev -o dev-requirements.txt pyproject.toml
pip-compile --extra docs -o docs-requirements.txt pyproject.toml
```

Pass `--extra` more than once to combine extras into one file:

```console
pip-compile --extra dev --extra docs -o tooling-requirements.txt pyproject.toml
```

`--all-extras` includes every declared extra:

```console
pip-compile --all-extras -o all-requirements.txt pyproject.toml
```

## When the project is in a different directory

```console
pip-compile -o requirements.txt path/to/project/pyproject.toml
```

The output file lands in the current directory. To put it next to the source, use a path:

```console
pip-compile -o path/to/project/requirements.txt path/to/project/pyproject.toml
```

## Static parse versus build-backend invocation

`pip-compile` reads `pyproject.toml` directly when the project metadata is fully declared (neither
`dependencies` nor `optional-dependencies` appears in `[project].dynamic`). This finishes in milliseconds.

When the project uses dynamic dependencies, `pip-compile` runs the project's PEP 517 backend in an
isolated environment to extract the metadata. This costs seconds. The first run pays the build-env
install cost; subsequent runs hit the wheel cache. {doc}`/explanation/build-system-integration` covers
the trade-off in detail.

You can confirm which path ran by passing `-v`:

```console
pip-compile -v -o requirements.txt pyproject.toml
```

The verbose log includes either "Statically parsed pyproject.toml" or backend-invocation lines.

## Self-referential extras

A project that imports its own extras inside another extra works without ceremony:

```toml
[project]
name = "my-app"
version = "1.0"
dependencies = ["django"]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = ["pytest"]
all = ["my-app[dev]"]
```

`pip-compile --extra all -o all-requirements.txt pyproject.toml` resolves `my-app[dev]` to the local
project, finds the `dev` extra, and pulls in `pytest`.

```{seealso}
- {doc}`compile-build-deps` adds the build-time dependencies to the output.
- {doc}`use-hashes` produces `--require-hashes`-mode output.
- {doc}`update-dependencies` bumps versions when you re-compile.
```
